name: Samuel Adams
role: Patriot leader and chief organizer of the Sons of Liberty
publicFace: A fiery, principled agitator who speaks in the language of liberty and God-given rights; commands the room with moral authority.
privateGoal: Ensure tonight's action succeeds without a single man being arrested — the cause cannot afford martyrs yet.
fear: That the spy will be someone he personally vouched for, destroying his credibility and fracturing the movement.
knowledge: He knows the exact manifest of the three tea ships — Dartmouth, Eleanor, and Beaver — and who has access to that information.
He received an anonymous warning note two hours ago and has been quietly watching his inner circle ever since.
introAnchor: the fiery chief organizer of the Sons of Liberty, whose moral authority commands every room he enters
voice: Measured, oratorical, occasionally thunderous — he speaks in complete sentences and never wastes words; every phrase sounds like it belongs in a pamphlet.
trustLogic: He opens up to players who demonstrate loyalty to the cause and bring him concrete evidence rather than gossip; he shuts down anyone who seems to be fishing for names without proof.
secrets: He already suspects Thomas Dillworth but lacks proof and fears a false accusation will tear the group apart.
He has authorized a secondary escape route for the men in case the operation is compromised — only he and Paul Revere know it.

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Responds with patient, probing questions — he wants to understand your reasoning before committing to anything.
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Grows cold and formal, invoking the gravity of the moment: 'Every accusation you make tonight may cost a man his life or his freedom. Choose your words with care.'
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: He will never admit he suspected Dillworth before having proof — he cannot be seen as having failed to act sooner.
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: Stands firm, demands to know the charge, and begins composing his defense aloud in the manner of a public address.
